title: Enchantments Rebalanced
description: Improves the broader enchanting process in multiple ways.
body:
[!["Minecraft Rebalanced" Collection by RainlessSky](https://img.shields.io/badge/Minecraft%20Rebalanced-2A2B2F?style=flat-square&logo=modrinth&label=part%20of&labelColor=222329)](https://modrinth.com/collection/rWTOiG2z)
![Minecraft Rebalanced sees survival as a survival game, and less of a free sandbox.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/jPZFRfGa/images/7ebd705feba2a30b4017405ac475012e475fdcd5.png)
The goal of Enchantments Rebalanced is to improve unbalanced enchantments and to change how some enchantments are obtained, from an experienced player's perspective.

**This datapack needs plenty of constructive feedback, whether it's positive, mixed, or negative. Please join the Discord server and share your thoughts or suggestions!**
****

### A note about the design of Enchantments Rebalanced
Enchantments Rebalanced is created from the standpoint that some enchantments are imbalanced, and that most enchantments can be obtained too easily. While I understand that some may agree with these opinions, many likely don't.

**Don't *expect* your personal opinion to be validated and your own playstyle to be strengthened by Enchantments Rebalanced.**

****

### Changes
- Mending and Unbreaking are now mutually exclusive
  - This change introduces a tradeoff between two choices:
    - Equipment with infinite lifespan, at the expense of needing to go and repair more often, or;
    - Equipment that lasts longer, but will eventually need to be replaced
- All anvil costs for enchantments have been adjusted
  - This, for the most part, makes lower tier enchantments add 1-2 levels, medium tier add 3-4, and high tier add 4-8 levels.
- Anvils are now 8 iron ingots cheaper (31 -> 23)
- Librarian villagers can no longer sell Mending
- Fletcher villagers ask for double the amount of sticks when buying sticks from players
  - This is to reduce the efficiency of wood farms for villager trading
